prompt: Inside the 'Eurovision Winner 2026' event with the contest only ~10 days out, show which national entries have the largest whale positions stacked behind them — total whale dollars per outcome, top single whale position size, whale count, and current YES probability for the top 8 nations by whale concentration.
expectedReason: prompt asks for an eight-item comparison
responsePreview:
As of May 5, 2026, whale conviction for the Eurovision Winner 2026 event is heavily concentrated in Finland and Greece. Finland leads with over $455,000 in whale-backed positions, more than double the next closest competitor.

The following table displays the top 8 nations by whale concentration:

| Nation | Current YES Probability | Total Whale Dollars | Top Single Whale Position | Whale Count |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Finland | 0.3535 | $455,887.48 | $194,288.45 | 25 |
| Greece | 0.1745 | $203,983.28 | $68,819.76 | 20 |
| Austria | 0.0025 | $2,780.85 | $1,250.00 | 1 |
| Estonia | 0.0025 | $2,747.34 | $1,169.28 | 1 |
| Armenia | 0.0025 | $2,732.70 | $968.64 | 0 |
| Albania | 0.0015 | $1,637.95 | $344.48 | 0 |
| Montenegro | 0.0015 | $1,636.78 | $237.61 | 0 |
| Georgia | 0.0015 | $1,635.41 | $416.61 | 0 |

The associated whale concentration data reveals a significant "long tail" effect. While Finland and Greece have "extreme" conviction levels with dozens of active whales, the remaining nations see a sharp drop-off, with very low probability and minimal large-scale position sizes.
